sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.121 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Let$p$be a prime number. A group$$is called if there exist constants$J$and$e$such that any finite subgroup$G$contains a normal abelian subgroup of order coprime to$p$and of index at most~ {$J |G_{(p)}|^e$}. (For example by the results of Brauer--Feit and Larsen--Pink, for any field$$of characteristic$p$the group$_n( )$is$p$-Jordan with$e=3$.) Let the ~$e( )$of the group$$be the infimum of all cons...
